question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause validate() to silently accept a fraudulent stream file by injecting VERIFIED signatures with an empty byte array as fileHash (getFileHashAsHex() returns empty string ''), grouping all such signatures under the same empty-string key in signatureHashMap, and accumulating enough stake to satisfy canReachConsensus(), bypassing the legitimate hash-based consensus check?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not verify that the totalStake reported by the first node equals the sum of all individual node stakes, by injecting a first-position signature with totalStake set to a value much larger than the actual sum of stakes, making stakeRequiredForConsensus permanently unachievable and causing a perpetual SignatureVerificationException that freezes fund processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a fund freeze by exploiting the fact that validate() only sets CONSENSUS_REACHED status on signatures in groups that pass canReachConsensus(), but never updates the status of signatures in failing groups, so that if all groups fail, all signatures remain in VERIFIED status and the importer's downstream processing incorrectly treats them as unprocessed, causing repeated re-validation attempts that always fail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: canReachConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a precision loss in BigDecimal.valueOf(stake).compareTo(stakeRequiredForConsensus) by crafting stake values near the boundary of the consensus threshold, where floating-point representation differences cause canReachConsensus() to return false even when the stake is mathematically sufficient, permanently blocking consensus and freezing fund ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a permanent fund freeze by injecting a stream signature file where the VERIFIED signatures are split across two hash groups, each with exactly half the required stake, so that neither group individually satisfies canReachConsensus() even though their combined stake would, and the importer never aggregates across groups, resulting in a perpetual SignatureVerificationException?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() uses the first signature's node totalStake for the entire validation, by racing to inject a signature from a node with a manipulated totalStake value at the head of the collection before legitimate signatures are processed, causing the consensus threshold to be computed incorrectly and permanently blocking stream file ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException in the second loop of validate() by ensuring stakeRequiredForConsensus is null (which happens when the signatures collection is non-empty but the first signature's node returns a totalStake that causes getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to throw before assignment), and then the exception propagates differently than expected, disrupting the importer's error recovery and causing a permanent halt?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the debugStake variable only tracks the maximum stake seen across hash groups (not the total), causing the error message in SignatureVerificationException to report a misleadingly low stake value that masks the true cause of consensus failure, making it harder for operators to detect and respond to an ongoing attack that is freezing fund processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a permanent fund freeze by exploiting the fact that validate() does not handle ConcurrentModificationException if the signatures collection is modified concurrently during iteration, by triggering a race condition where another thread modifies the collection while validate() is iterating, causing an exception that permanently halts the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not validate that all signatures in the collection belong to the same stream file (same filename), by injecting signatures from different stream files into the same collection, causing the consensus check to be performed on a mixed set of signatures that can never reach consensus for any single file, permanently blocking ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a permanent fund freeze by ensuring that the source bucket (S3/GCS) always returns signature files where fewer than 1/3 of nodes have uploaded their signatures, so that validate() always receives an insufficient number of VERIFIED signatures and throws SignatureVerificationException on every cycle, permanently stalling the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not check for duplicate node IDs in the signatures collection, allowing an attacker to inject multiple VERIFIED signatures claiming to be from the same high-stake node with different fileHash values, splitting that node's stake across multiple hash groups so that no single group reaches the consensus threshold, permanently blocking stream file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a permanent fund freeze by exploiting the fact that validate() does not enforce a minimum number of signatures before attempting consensus, so that a single VERIFIED signature from a node with stake equal to totalStake (100% stake) would pass canReachConsensus() and mark itself as CONSENSUS_REACHED, allowing a single compromised or spoofed node to unilaterally determine consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the signatureHashMap is a HashMultimap (not a TreeMultimap or LinkedHashMultimap), so the iteration order over signatureHashMap.keySet() is non-deterministic, causing the debugStake tracking to report different values across runs for the same input, making it impossible to reliably diagnose why consensus is failing and prolonging a fund freeze attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a permanent fund freeze by injecting a StreamFileSignature whose getStatus() method throws a RuntimeException when called, causing the status comparison (signature.getStatus() == StreamFileSignature.SignatureStatus.VERIFIED) to abort with an unhandled exception that propagates out of validate() and permanently halts the importer's download cycle?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not cross-check the totalStake value from the first signature against the sum of all individual node stakes in the collection, by injecting a first-position signature with totalStake=Long.MAX_VALUE, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to compute a threshold of Long.MAX_VALUE * consensusRatio that no real stake accumulation can ever reach, permanently freezing fund processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a permanent fund freeze by exploiting the fact that validate() does not retry or fall back when SignatureVerificationException is thrown, and the Downloader.downloadNextBatch() only logs a warning before the next scheduled poll, so that by continuously maintaining a state where consensus cannot be reached, the attacker can keep the importer permanently stalled without any automatic recovery mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() uses BigDecimal arithmetic with RoundingMode.CEILING for the threshold but plain long arithmetic for stake accumulation, creating a systematic off-by-one bias where the threshold is always rounded up while the accumulated stake is always truncated, causing legitimate consensus groups to fail canReachConsensus() by exactly 1 unit in edge cases and permanently blocking fund processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a permanent fund freeze by exploiting the fact that validate() does not validate the SignatureStatus transition (a signature should not already be in CONSENSUS_REACHED status when passed to validate()), allowing an attacker to pre-set signatures to CONSENSUS_REACHED status so they are excluded from the VERIFIED check, reducing the effective number of verified signatures below the consensus threshold?
